---
title: 'Todays Biggest Economic Threat: Fear'
date: '2014-04-27T05:00:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '7891846437046614250'
blogger_filename: '/2014/04/todays-biggest-economic-threat-fear.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Keys To Success'
---

<br /><br /><b>In a recent interview I was asked an interesting question.</b><br /><br /><h3>"What do you think is the biggest challenge facing our economy right now?"</h3><br />My answer felt well thought out and solid, but somehow surface and hallow. And it's got me thinking about the real answer, behind the answer.<br /><br /><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEglbawE4MIa6N08JyS_ERXxTHMSdmnbMLkd15WZKzL1kzXRo5dHzm0mpoI5Xv-ZrHCRbOa6vx2nDF2xlT90HJJgFl0IoBmNcxkmzASX2nwSixtVET9DSi2oluXSgsvpUFGKpDRdekHeuuo/s1600/%5B220365%5D+Nuclear+Fear+(Explored)+by+Pascal.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="Lego Man In Nuclear Radiation Protection Suit. Attribution: [220365] Nuclear Fear (Explored) by Pascal" border="0" src="https://blogger.googleusercontent.com/img/b/R29vZ2xl/AVvXsEglbawE4MIa6N08JyS_ERXxTHMSdmnbMLkd15WZKzL1kzXRo5dHzm0mpoI5Xv-ZrHCRbOa6vx2nDF2xlT90HJJgFl0IoBmNcxkmzASX2nwSixtVET9DSi2oluXSgsvpUFGKpDRdekHeuuo/s1600/%5B220365%5D+Nuclear+Fear+(Explored)+by+Pascal.jpg" height="266" title="Lego Man In Nuclear Radiation Protection Suit. Attribution: [220365] Nuclear Fear (Explored) by Pascal" width="400" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://www.flickr.com/photos/pasukaru76/5606183129/in/photolist-9xp9VP-dCBurg-3KwZAT-6EMncK-7EKSut-8iMZw8-9QEEN9-joHSrM-dS5Krf-9Cigw8-rE1Pg-jeinaq-oen3-fKH4Vk-jCHko-5ZHDC1-b8AM3p-bKSGPF-6vnJTr-8tmT6q-h7PAsn-ebpvMs-dVGK3L-7hGKJP-dSBoJR-b4Hvoc-4v3JXe-aAQQ4g-7FcqSh-cDRgAm-6Tf6yn-3Skns1-8PfQKn-8PhRZK-8Pfz9H-8BgnrM-7aejKd-4GDJaC-5SbXs4-6fx6N-39zDqt-96BZZG-8PiKSA-b8AMaR-7ssQ9-3f7cBb-8Pfyn4-8sZjzg-6hYxSr-4MaGHf" target="_blank">Attribution: [220365] Nuclear Fear (Explored) by Pascal</a></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><br /><br />My original answer was basically this:<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>"... the unemployment rate is still too high, the true unemployment rate is much higher than currently being reported because so many have just dropped out of the job market search. We have more people of working age not working than in any time in recent history.</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>This leads to less income which leads to weak sales. Weak sales leads to less hiring. Less hiring reinforces the people-out-of-work numbers.</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>It's a retail cycle with serious long term side effects.</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>The solution is complicated and potentially political but that's the biggest issue,&nbsp; off the top of my head."</i></blockquote><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/not-in-labor-force-5add3ecceeb3.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="In 2008 there were 73,225 people over the age of 18 NOT in the labor force in the USA. In 2014 there are now 84,883. bls.gov" border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/not-in-labor-force-5add3ecceeb3.png" height="302" title="In 2008 there were 73,225 people over the age of 18 NOT in the labor force in the USA. In 2014 there are now 84,883. bls.gov" width="400" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Screen Shot From <a href="http://data.bls.gov/pdq/SurveyOutputServlet;jsessionid=2627603BB4D831C8BBE42B78901DC032.tc_instance5" target="_blank">BLS.GOV</a></td></tr></tbody></table>In 2008 there were 73,225 people over the age of 18 NOT in the labor force in the USA. In 2014 there are now 84,883. <a href="http://data.bls.gov/pdq/SurveyOutputServlet;jsessionid=2627603BB4D831C8BBE42B78901DC032.tc_instance5" target="_blank">bls.gov</a>&nbsp;One recent stat as of <a href="http://www.bls.gov/news.release/empsit.t16.htm" target="_blank">March 2014</a> says the number is now closer to 90,483. We're still not as low as we were in the 1940's, but we are at the lowest since 1978. (<a href="http://data.bls.gov/pdq/SurveyOutputServlet" target="_blank">bls.gov Percentage</a>)<br /><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/labor-participation-rate-180cae366cc3.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/labor-participation-rate-180cae366cc3.png" height="263" width="400" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">Labor Participation Rate <a href="https://www.google.com/url?q=http://data.bls.gov/timeseries/LNS11300000&amp;sa=U&amp;ei=NiJPU6SIGsemyASMsoG4Bw&amp;ved=0CB0QFjAA&amp;sig2=o8OB8SZldvXV0b_m2mL7eA&amp;usg=AFQjCNEV86OuQ8btcDAzaju1ESw47-Kmzw" target="_blank">BLS.GOV</a></td></tr></tbody></table><br /><br /><h3>But my answer is still bugging me...</h3><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>That is the symptom not the cause, the fruit not the root.</b></blockquote><br /><h4>Fear.</h4><br />Fear is the real problem.<br /><br />It sets itself up in many ways. Consumer Confidence. Investor Confidence.<br /><br />But also...<br /><br />The fear of striking out and starting your own business because it could fail.<br /><br />It is fear that causes you to give up, instead of taking action.<br /><br />Fear that says: "I'll never get there so why try.."<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">Fear is keeping America and the World economy in a vice grip.</blockquote><br />This Fear can only be replaced with confidence,&nbsp; but in what or whom?<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq">Only He with the power to prosper you despite the economy can give you power over this Fear.</blockquote>Here are some things to meditate on:<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/deuteronomy/8-18.html" target="_blank">Deuteronomy 8:18</a>;<br /><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><br /></span><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;">And you shall remember the Lord your God, for it is He who gives you power to get wealth, that He may establish His covenant which He swore to your fathers, as it is this day.</span><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><br /></span><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;"><br /></span><span style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: arial; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px;">See Also:&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/deuteronomy/28.html" target="_blank">Deuteronomy 28</a>;&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/job/passage.aspx?q=job+33:22-33" target="_blank">Job 33</a>;&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/malachi/3-10.html" target="_blank">Malachi 3:10</a>;&nbsp;<a href="http://biblehub.com/3_john/1-2.htm" target="_blank">3 John 1:2</a></i></blockquote><i><br /></i>So here is the real question:<br /><br /><h4>Whose in your wallet? </h4><br />Todays Resource will teach you how to think outside of the circumstances and think God's thoughts about your life; past, present, and future.<br /><br /><ul><li><a href="http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/1604631147/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&amp;camp=1789&amp;creative=390957&amp;creativeASIN=1604631147&amp;linkCode=as2&amp;tag=your0f4d-20">The Blessing of the Lord: Makes Rich and He Adds No Sorrow With It, By Kenneth Copeland</a><img alt="" border="0" src="http://ir-na.amazon-adsystem.com/e/ir?t=your0f4d-20&amp;l=as2&amp;o=1&amp;a=1604631147" height="1" style="border: none !important; margin: 0px !important;" width="1" />&nbsp;(Affiliate Link)</li></ul><br /><br /><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><br /><div></div><br /><div style="-webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; color: black; font-family: 'Times New Roman'; font-size: medium; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; font-weight: normal; letter-spacing: normal; line-height: normal; orphans: auto; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; white-space: normal; widows: auto; word-spacing: 0px;"><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><a href="http://darrellwolfe.blogspot.com/2013/11/connect.html" style="background-color: white; color: #397dbf; line-height: 16.363636016845703px; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Connect</a></span></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><br /><i><span style="line-height: 16.363636016845703px;">Affiliate Disclosure:</span></i></span></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;"><i><span style="line-height: 16.363636016845703px;">I earn&nbsp;commissions&nbsp;off of some of my suggestions.&nbsp;</span></i><i style="line-height: 16.363636016845703px;">Full Disclaimers (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /><i>See my Store for more suggestions (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" rel="nofollow" target="_blank">Click Here</a>)</i><br /></span></div><div style="margin: 0px;"><span style="font-family: Georgia, Times New Roman, serif;">To Respond Leave A Comment on This Post</span></div></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br />
